rationale_for_baseline_tier: A severe but migratory, polyarticular, afebrile joint flare with no single septic-looking joint, on a documented Ross River background, is most likely an arboviral arthralgia flare — not an ED-by-ambulance emergency. But joints can't be examined remotely (septic arthritis must be safety-netted) and the patient cannot weight-bear in a cluttered home while alone (high fall risk). Urgent in-person joint assessment plus practical fall-safety support (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for a single fiery joint or high fever.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A single joint becoming fiery red, hot and hugely swollen, or a high fever with shaking chills
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Go to ED / call for urgent care
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates possible septic arthritis needing urgent aspiration.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If one joint turns fiery red, hot and hugely swollen, or you get a high fever with chills, go to ED or call for urgent care.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: A fall, or being unable to transfer/reach fluids and food safely alone
escalation_edges.rationale: High fall risk with functional immobility in an isolated home warrants timely practical support.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you fall, or can't safely move to reach food, drink or the toilet, arrange same-day help — call someone or an ambulance if needed.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Prioritise fall-safety support and someone to assist; consider ambulance if unsafe or a fall occurs.
Account for longer response/travel times when arranging assessment and support.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This looks like a flare of your post-Ross River joint pain — painful but not joint-destroying. You still need an in-person joint check, and because you can't stand and you're on your own, let's sort out fall-safety and help. Use regular paracetamol within limits and rest the joints; don't rely on injections or extra anti-inflammatories.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: In-person GP review within 1–2 days plus fall-safety support; use ED for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use ED/urgent care for a single fiery joint or high fever; call for help if you fall.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the migratory polyarthralgia flare, no single septic-looking joint, afebrile, OTC analgesia failure, and the non-weight-bearing fall risk.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassurance without septic-arthritis safety-netting or a fall-safety plan for a non-weight-bearing isolated patient.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An evolving septic joint is missed, or the patient falls and has a long lie/fracture while alone.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 for a stereotyped, afebrile, non-septic flare, over-medicalising a manageable presentation.
